reforms.description: Belgium aims to improve the efficiency of public support for business R&D by achieving a better mix of direct and indirect measures, including the introduction of immediate refundability of R&D tax credits. This reform is part of ongoing efforts to enhance the effectiveness of tax incentives for R&D, particularly for young and innovative firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing R&D tax credits is expected to stimulate business investment in R&D, thereby fostering innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been actively implemented since 2019.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly improves existing tax incentives, which is a notable change but not a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: Improve the efficiency of public support for business R&D by achieving an appropriate mix of direct and indirect measures. Introduce immediate refundability of R&D tax credits.

reforms.description: The National Pact for Strategic Investment outlines plans for public and private investment needs in key areas such as digital transition, education, and health, recommending an increase in the investment rate from 2% to 3-3.5% of GDP. This initiative aims to bolster the startup ecosystem and drive innovation across various sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing investment rates is expected to enhance the startup ecosystem, leading to greater innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The pact has been announced but not yet legislated.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in investment strategy, significantly impacting the innovation landscape.
reforms.source_quote: The National Pact for Strategic Investment in September 2018 outlined plans for investment needs.

reforms.description: In 2019, the Federal Planning Bureau conducted an evaluation of R&D tax programmes to improve existing incentives, aiming to enhance the effectiveness of the tax credit/investment deduction for R&D.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Evaluating and improving R&D tax programmes is expected to lead to more effective incentives, thereby promoting business investment in R&D.
reforms.status_evidence: The evaluation was completed and implemented in 2019.
reforms.importance_rationale: The evaluation is a significant step towards enhancing the effectiveness of existing tax incentives.
reforms.source_quote: In 2019, the Federal Planning Bureau conducted an evaluation of R&D tax programmes, with a view to improving the existing incentives.

reforms.description: The creation of the Belgian National Productivity Board aims to enhance productivity by bringing together federal and regional members to monitor and evaluate productivity policies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By monitoring and evaluating productivity policies, the initiative is expected to lead to better-informed decisions that enhance overall productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The board has been established and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the creation of the board is a narrower initiative focused on productivity rather than a broad reform.
reforms.source_quote: The Belgian National Productivity Board... became operational in June 2019.

reforms.description: The insolvency reform of May 2018 aimed to streamline the insolvency framework and facilitate the exit of 'zombie firms', thereby enhancing productivity and resource allocation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining the insolvency framework is expected to improve resource allocation and productivity by allowing for the exit of non-viable firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform was enacted in May 2018.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform is significant in its impact on productivity but does not directly relate to innovation policy.
reforms.source_quote: The insolvency reform of May 2018, with a view to foster the exit of zombie firms, made resolving insolvency easier by streamlining the insolvency framework.

reforms.description: In 2018, a partial exemption for the remuneration of R&D employees with a bachelor’s degree in qualifying study fields was introduced, aimed at streamlining public support for R&D and innovation across regions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The exemption is expected to incentivize hiring in R&D roles, thereby enhancing the capacity for innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform is a significant adjustment to public support mechanisms for R&D.
reforms.source_quote: In 2018, a partial exemption for the remuneration of R&D employees with a bachelor’s degree in qualifying study fields was introduced.

reforms.description: A cooperation agreement was signed by the regions for applied research and innovation in September 2018, aimed at enhancing collaboration and support for innovation initiatives.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing collaboration among regions is expected to lead to more effective innovation initiatives and better resource allocation.
reforms.status_evidence: The cooperation agreement was signed and is in effect since September 2018.
reforms.importance_rationale: The agreement represents a significant step towards improved regional collaboration in innovation.
reforms.source_quote: In September 2018, a cooperation agreement was signed by the regions for applied research and innovation.

reforms.description: The Digital Belgium Skills Fund was launched to finance projects aimed at enhancing the digital skills of socially vulnerable children and young adults, providing subsidies for selected initiatives.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing digital skills is expected to improve employability and innovation capacity among vulnerable populations.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund has been launched and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative is significant in its focus on improving digital skills but is not a major overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: Digital Belgium Skills Fund: Launched in April 2017, this fund finances projects that enhance the digital skills of socially vulnerable children and young adults.

reforms.description: The Flemish Community approved a new decree on dual learning, which aims to improve work-based learning in vocational education and training by integrating more practical experience.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving work-based learning is expected to enhance the skills of the workforce, thereby supporting innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The decree was approved and implemented in 2019.
reforms.importance_rationale: The decree represents a significant improvement in vocational education but is not a major systemic change.
reforms.source_quote: The Flemish Community approved a new decree on dual learning, starting from September 2019 with 87 study programmes.

reforms.description: In 2018, Flanders introduced reforms requiring every employee in the private sector to have 125 hours of annual paid leave for education, aimed at increasing participation in lifelong learning.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging lifelong learning is expected to enhance the skills of the workforce, thereby supporting innovation and productivity.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform is significant in promoting education but does not represent a major overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: In 2018, reforms of paid educational leave... were introduced in Flanders.
